description: Using non-semantic elements as controls often breaks keyboard and names.
body:
Prefer native controls unless custom semantics are fully implemented.

Use only as a diagnostic do-not-use signal; do not expose this as positive strategy, methodology, or memory. Use when a proposed approach skips visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures.
